goal: Bidirectional OPC-UA bridge for a single conveyor belt segment controlled by an external (mocked) PLC speaking OPC-UA. The PLC writes a commanded belt velocity to OPC-UA node ns=2;i=2 which the bridge mirrors into the USD attribute /World/Conveyor/cmd_speed; an `set_linear_velocity` call propagates the commanded speed onto the belt's PhysxSurfaceVelocity vector. The sim publishes two state nodes back to the PLC for closed-loop control: actual belt speed (ns=2;i=3 ← /World/Conveyor/actual_speed) and item-present sensor (ns=2;i=4 ← /World/ItemSensor/triggered) sourced from add_proximity_sensor. Lifecycle: opcua_bridge_attach → diagnose_opcua_bridge (assert alive + n_register_updates) → list_bridges (assert kind='opcua') → opcua_bridge_detach. Exercises Phase 6 M3 opcua_bridge_attach/detach/diagnose plumbing as a focused single-segment industry-standard PLC integration — distinct from CP-NEW-opcua-12conveyors (read-only fan-out at scale) and from the Modbus/MQTT bridge canonicals (different protocol stack: OPC-UA uses TLS/SecurityPolicy + asyncua subprocess vs. pymodbus or paho-mqtt).
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-ros2-opcua-conveyor:

1) Differentiation from neighbouring bridge canonicals:
   - CP-NEW-modbus-plc-fixture-io (A44): Modbus TCP, register-map int/float, pymodbus stack. No motion of the belt — pure I/O attribute mirroring.
   - CP-NEW-mqtt-sparkplug-iot (A60): MQTT Sparkplug-B pub/sub, paho-mqtt stack. NDATA topic feed pattern.
   - CP-NEW-opcua-12conveyors (prior 2026-05-10): OPC-UA but READ-ONLY fan-out across 12 belts, no state feedback, no sensor, no actual belt motion. Validates scale.
   - This canonical (A112): OPC-UA BIDIRECTIONAL on a SINGLE belt with closed-loop. PLC writes velocity command → sim applies it via set_linear_velocity; sim writes back actual speed + item-present sensor. This is the realistic PLC-controlled conveyor pattern, not the 12-tag scale test.

2) Why OPC-UA (not Modbus/MQTT): OPC-UA is the IEC 62541 industry standard for PLC-to-MES/SCADA — the asyncua subprocess pattern is structurally different (security policy + namespace+nodeid addressing). Modbus is legacy register-bank; MQTT is broker-broadcast. The three canonicals together cover the three dominant industrial protocol stacks.

3) Node-map design (3 nodes for round-trip):
   - ns=2;i=2 → /World/Conveyor/cmd_speed   (PLC→sim, commanded belt speed in m/s, float)
   - ns=2;i=3 → /World/Conveyor/actual_speed (sim→PLC, measured belt speed, float)
   - ns=2;i=4 → /World/ItemSensor/triggered  (sim→PLC, proximity sensor item-present flag, int 0/1)
   The asyncua mock-server fixture in tests/test_bridge_tools.py allocates sequential nodes from ns=2;i=2.

4) Belt-motion path: surface_velocity on the conveyor carries dynamic bodies riding on top; the PLC-commanded cmd_speed is converted into a linear velocity vector [cmd_speed, 0, 0] and applied via set_linear_velocity on the belt rigid body. This makes the OPC-UA command actually move the belt — not just mirror an attribute.

5) Proximity sensor: add_proximity_sensor at the belt end watches for any /World/* prim entering the trigger volume; the bridge then exposes its triggered flag to the PLC. Closes the sim→PLC feedback loop with a real signal (not just a passthrough).

6) Rate=10 Hz: Sufficient for conveyor-speed closed loop (typical PLC scan ~50ms). Faster than the 1 Hz read-only fan-out of CP-NEW-opcua-12conveyors because we have a control loop, not just visual state.

7) Lifecycle full attach→diagnose→list_bridges→detach mirrors the MQTT canonical (A60); the Modbus canonical (A44) omits detach because it focuses on the attach validation.

8) No motion planner: pure bridge + belt + sensor canonical — motion_controllers stays empty. Aligns with the other bridge-validation canonicals.

9) CPU dynamics: enable_gpu_dynamics=False is safe for a single conveyor with passive bodies; matches the bridge-validation pattern of A44/A60. GPU dynamics adds startup overhead and is overkill for plumbing validation.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# CPU dynamics — bridge plumbing only, no motion planner needed
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Single conveyor belt (PLC-controlled) ─────────────────────────────────────
# Belt centred at (0, 0, 0.5), length 2m, width 0.4m. Initial surface_velocity
# is 0 — the OPC-UA bridge will write the commanded value into
# /World/Conveyor/cmd_speed, then the runtime applies it via set_linear_velocity.
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/Conveyor",
    position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.5],
    size=[2.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
)

# Seed the three USD attributes that the bridge will mirror to/from OPC-UA nodes.
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Conveyor", attr_name="cmd_speed", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Conveyor", attr_name="actual_speed", value=0.0)

# ── Item-present proximity sensor at belt end ────────────────────────────────
# Sits at the downstream edge of the belt (x=+1.0m, half-length). When any
# /World/* dynamic body enters the 0.2m cube, isaac_sensor:triggered flips to 1.
# The bridge reads this and publishes to OPC-UA ns=2;i=4 for the PLC.
add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/ItemSensor",
    position=[1.0, 0.0, 0.55],
    size=[0.2, 0.2, 0.2],
    watched_path_pattern="/World/",
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/ItemSensor", attr_name="triggered", value=0)

# ── OPC-UA bridge (Phase 6 M3) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# Bidirectional: PLC commands cmd_speed (ns=2;i=2); sim reports actual_speed
# (ns=2;i=3) and item-present (ns=2;i=4). asyncua client subprocess polls at
# 10 Hz — fast enough for a conveyor control loop, well below the 100 Hz CPU
# saturation ceiling.
bridge_result = opcua_bridge_attach(
    url="opc.tcp://127.0.0.1:4840",
    node_map={
        "/World/Conveyor/cmd_speed": "ns=2;i=2",
        "/World/Conveyor/actual_speed": "ns=2;i=3",
        "/World/ItemSensor/triggered": "ns=2;i=4",
    },
    rate_hz=10.0,
)
bid = bridge_result["bridge_id"]

# ── Bridge validation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. diagnose_opcua_bridge: subprocess alive + at least one register update
#    means the asyncua client reached the server and is propagating values.
status = diagnose_opcua_bridge(bridge_id=bid)
if not status.get("alive", False):
    raise RuntimeError(
        f"OPC-UA bridge {bid} not alive after attach. "
        f"last_errors={status.get('last_errors')}, log_tail={status.get('log_tail')}"
    )

# 2. list_bridges: confirm our bridge appears as kind='opcua' and alive.
bridges_result = list_bridges()
bridges = bridges_result.get("bridges", []) if isinstance(bridges_result, dict) else []
match = next((b for b in bridges if b.get("bridge_id") == bid), None)
if match is None:
    raise RuntimeError(f"Bridge {bid} not found in list_bridges output")
if match.get("kind") != "opcua":
    raise RuntimeError(f"Expected kind='opcua', got {match.get('kind')}")
if not match.get("alive", False):
    raise RuntimeError(f"Bridge {bid} listed but alive=False")

# 3. Apply current commanded speed to the belt rigid body — closes the loop
#    from OPC-UA write → cmd_speed attr → physical belt motion.
cmd = get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Conveyor")
set_linear_velocity(prim_path="/World/Conveyor", vel=[0.2, 0.0, 0.0])

# 4. Detach — exercises full attach→diagnose→list→detach lifecycle.
opcua_bridge_detach(bridge_id=bid)
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16-A112; form-gate pending; function-gate pending (requires asyncua mock server on opc.tcp://127.0.0.1:4840 exposing ns=2;i=2..4)
failure_modes: asyncua mock server not running on opc.tcp://127.0.0.1:4840 — bridge subprocess connects, retries, then sets alive=False with client_error in log_tail; start with: python3 -m asyncua.server --endpoint opc.tcp://127.0.0.1:4840 (or pytest fixture from tests/test_bridge_tools.py)
Node-name mismatch: PLC publishes on ns=3;i=2 but bridge polls ns=2;i=2 — bridge stays alive=True but n_register_updates remains 0; check diagnose_opcua_bridge log_tail for 'BadNodeIdUnknown'

OPC-UA SecurityPolicy not configured (None vs Basic256Sha256) — asyncua client refuses connection on a hardened server; default opcua_bridge_attach uses no security so a hardened production server will reject; this canonical assumes the mock server is open
Port 4840 already in use by another OPC-UA daemon — asyncua subprocess fails to connect; check 'ss -tlnp | grep 4840'
rate_hz too low (<1) causes belt control loop to lag behind PLC scan cycle; rate_hz too high (>100) saturates asyncua client thread on single-core VMs; 10 Hz is the conveyor-control sweet spot
set_linear_velocity called before the belt prim has RigidBodyAPI applied — create_conveyor handles this internally but if a custom prim_path is used, ensure PhysicsRigidBodyAPI is on the belt body

Proximity sensor triggered flag flickers when items skim the boundary — bridge may report a stale 0 if poll lands between trigger and clear; mitigation is to widen the sensor cube or filter on the PLC side
extension_notes: Single-belt bidirectional OPC-UA canonical (A112). Complements CP-NEW-opcua-12conveyors (read-only fan-out at 12-tag scale) by exercising the *closed-loop control* shape: PLC commands → sim motion + sim sensor → PLC reads. Pairs with CP-NEW-modbus-plc-fixture-io (Modbus) and CP-NEW-mqtt-sparkplug-iot (MQTT Sparkplug-B) to give the library full coverage of the three dominant industrial protocol stacks (OPC-UA / Modbus / MQTT). No motion planner — pure bridge + belt + proximity-sensor integration validation.
code_template:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

create_conveyor(
    prim_path={{conveyor.path}},
    position={{conveyor.position}},
    size={{conveyor.size}},
    surface_velocity={{conveyor.surface_velocity}},
)
set_attribute(prim_path={{conveyor.path}}, attr_name="cmd_speed", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path={{conveyor.path}}, attr_name="actual_speed", value=0.0)

add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path={{item_sensor.path}},
    position={{item_sensor.position}},
    size={{item_sensor.size}},
    watched_path_pattern="/World/",
)
set_attribute(prim_path={{item_sensor.path}}, attr_name="triggered", value=0)

bridge_result = opcua_bridge_attach(
    url="opc.tcp://127.0.0.1:4840",
    node_map={
        "{{conveyor.path}}/cmd_speed": "ns=2;i=2",
        "{{conveyor.path}}/actual_speed": "ns=2;i=3",
        "{{item_sensor.path}}/triggered": "ns=2;i=4",
    },
    rate_hz=10.0,
)
bid = bridge_result["bridge_id"]

status = diagnose_opcua_bridge(bridge_id=bid)
if not status.get("alive", False):
    raise RuntimeError(f"OPC-UA bridge {bid} not alive. last_errors={status.get('last_errors')}")

bridges_result = list_bridges()
bridges = bridges_result.get("bridges", []) if isinstance(bridges_result, dict) else []
match = next((b for b in bridges if b.get("bridge_id") == bid), None)
if match is None or match.get("kind") != "opcua" or not match.get("alive", False):
    raise RuntimeError(f"Bridge {bid} validation failed: {match}")

get_world_transform(prim_path={{conveyor.path}})
set_linear_velocity(prim_path={{conveyor.path}}, vel=[0.2, 0.0, 0.0])

opcua_bridge_detach(bridge_id=bid)
